title: Business Process AI-Impact Analysis
subtitle: How each of the Energy Client's core business processes can benefit from AI agents — grounded in the ArchiMate repository with linked roles, applications, and data objects

value_streams.name: Order-to-Delivery
value_streams.description: End-to-end value stream from customer order through engineering, manufacturing, testing, and delivery
value_streams.mermaid:
graph LR
  P002["Transformer\nDesign"]
  P003["Material\nProcurement"]
  P004["Transformer\nAssembly"]
  P005["Factory Acceptance\nTesting"]
  P006["Heavy Transport\n& Delivery"]
  P002 --> P003 --> P004 --> P005 --> P006
  classDef high fill:#fef2f2,stroke:#dc2626,color:#991b1b
  classDef medium fill:#fffbeb,stroke:#d97706,color:#92400e
  class P002,P003,P005 high
  class P004,P006 medium

ai_impact.summary: End-to-end orchestration with AI-optimized design, predictive procurement, and autonomous quality gates
ai_impact.current_state: Manually coordinated 9-14 month cycle with sequential handoffs between engineering, procurement, manufacturing, and logistics teams
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI orchestration agent manages the full value chain — generative design reduces cycle by 30%, predictive procurement prevents delays, computer vision automates quality checks, route optimization for heavy transport

ai_impact.summary: Generative design exploration with AI co-pilot for transformer engineering
ai_impact.current_state: Expert engineers use proprietary TDS software with 30+ years of accumulated rules — each design takes 2-6 weeks of iterative calculation
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: Generative AI explores design space against constraints (IEC standards, thermal limits, material costs), proposes optimized designs for engineer review, auto-generates BOM and 3D models
barriers: Proprietary TDS codebase is hard to integrate

ai_impact.summary: Autonomous procurement agent handles sourcing, negotiation, and order placement for standard materials
ai_impact.current_state: Procurement managers manually source copper, core steel, insulation oil from approved suppliers — 4-8 week lead times, price volatility on commodities
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI agent monitors commodity markets, predicts price movements, auto-negotiates with pre-qualified suppliers via Ariba, triggers purchase orders when optimal conditions met

ai_impact.summary: AI-guided assembly with real-time quality monitoring and adaptive scheduling
ai_impact.current_state: Skilled workers follow work instructions across 8-12 week assembly cycles — manual quality checks at defined gates, production scheduling via MES
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: Computer vision monitors assembly quality in real-time, digital twin tracks progress vs plan, AI adjusts production schedule dynamically based on supply chain status and workforce availability
barriers: Skilled workforce resistance to AI oversight

ai_impact.summary: AI-powered test analysis with automated anomaly detection and certificate generation
ai_impact.current_state: Manual test execution over 1-2 weeks per unit — engineers analyze waveforms, partial discharge patterns, and thermal data against IEC 60076 requirements
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: ML models analyze test data in real-time, flag anomalies automatically, compare against fleet-wide historical patterns, auto-generate test certificates and reports
barriers: IEC certification requirements for AI-assisted testing

ai_impact.summary: Autonomous route planning and permit orchestration for heavy transformer transport
ai_impact.current_state: Logistics specialists manually plan routes for 200-400 ton transformer shipments — bridge clearances, road permits, police escorts, port coordination
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI agent plans optimal routes considering weight restrictions, bridge ratings, weather, permits in parallel, real-time tracking with automated stakeholder notifications
ai_impact.estimated_value: 30% faster permit processing, 20% logistics cost reduction

value_streams.name: Incident-to-Resolution
value_streams.description: Grid incident detection through fault analysis, crew dispatch, and full service restoration
value_streams.mermaid:
graph LR
  P008["Fault Detection\n& Isolation"]
  P009["Fault Analysis\n& Root Cause"]
  P010["Service\nRestoration"]
  P008 --> P009 --> P010
  classDef high fill:#fef2f2,stroke:#dc2626,color:#991b1b
  classDef medium fill:#fffbeb,stroke:#d97706,color:#92400e
  class P008,P009 high
  class P010 medium

ai_impact.summary: Near-autonomous fault management from detection through analysis to coordinated restoration
ai_impact.current_state: Operators monitor SCADA dashboards, manually analyze alarm cascades, coordinate switching operations with field crews via radio/phone
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI agent processes SCADA events in real-time, performs instant root cause analysis using graph-based grid model, recommends optimal switching sequence, auto-dispatches crews with mobile work orders
barriers: Safety regulations require human approval for switching
Varying SCADA system maturity across utilities

ai_impact.summary: ML-powered fault detection reduces identification time from minutes to seconds
ai_impact.current_state: SCADA alarms trigger operator attention — manual correlation of protection relay events, alarm flooding during major faults obscures root cause
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: ML model trained on historical fault signatures processes relay data and SCADA events in real-time, identifies fault type and location within seconds, filters alarm noise automatically
ai_impact.estimated_value: 95% faster fault identification, 80% alarm reduction during cascading events
barriers: Model training requires labeled historical fault data

ai_impact.summary: Graph-based causal analysis identifies root causes using grid topology and event correlation
ai_impact.current_state: Protection engineers manually review event sequences, disturbance recordings, and fault waveforms — analysis can take hours for complex cascading faults
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI traverses grid topology graph to trace fault propagation, correlates protection relay sequences with physical grid model, generates root cause report with confidence scores

ai_impact.summary: AI recommends optimal restoration sequence while human operators approve critical switching
ai_impact.current_state: Operators plan switching sequence manually, coordinate with field crews and neighboring grid operators, execute step-by-step with safety checks
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI generates optimal restoration plan considering load priorities, equipment ratings, and crew availability — presents ranked options to operator for approval, auto-dispatches field crews
ai_impact.estimated_value: 40% faster restoration, optimized load shedding decisions

value_streams.name: Maintain-to-Optimize
value_streams.description: Maintenance planning through execution to asset performance optimization and lifecycle extension
value_streams.mermaid:
graph LR
  P012["Sensor Data\nCollection"]
  P013["Condition\nAssessment"]
  P014["Work Order\nGeneration"]
  P012 --> P013 --> P014
  classDef high fill:#fef2f2,stroke:#dc2626,color:#991b1b
  classDef medium fill:#fffbeb,stroke:#d97706,color:#92400e
  class P013,P014 high
  class P012 medium

ai_impact.summary: Fully autonomous maintenance cycle from data collection through analysis to work order generation
ai_impact.current_state: Periodic condition assessments with manual data review — maintenance often reactive or calendar-based rather than condition-based
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: Autonomous agent continuously monitors asset health, runs predictive models, generates and prioritizes work orders, optimizes maintenance windows across fleet
ai_impact.estimated_value: 30-50% reduction in unplanned outages, 25% maintenance cost reduction
barriers: Sensor coverage gaps on older assets
Model accuracy for rare failure modes

ai_impact.summary: Automated data pipeline with AI-driven quality checks and adaptive sampling rates
ai_impact.current_state: Fixed-interval sensor polling via RTUs and IoT gateways — data flows through OPC-UA to Lumada data lake, manual data quality checks
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI adjusts sampling rates based on asset condition (increase frequency when degradation detected), auto-validates data quality, fills gaps with interpolation, flags sensor failures
ai_impact.estimated_value: 40% reduction in data quality issues, 60% storage optimization

ai_impact.summary: ML models continuously score asset health and predict remaining useful life
ai_impact.current_state: Engineers periodically review dissolved gas analysis, vibration, and temperature data — manual health scoring using industry standards (IEEE C57.104)
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: ML ensemble combines DGA, vibration, thermal, and electrical data into real-time health score, predicts RUL with confidence intervals, detects incipient faults months before failure
ai_impact.estimated_value: 85% failure prediction accuracy, 6-month advance warning for major failures
barriers: Limited failure data for rare transformer types

ai_impact.summary: Autonomous work order creation with intelligent prioritization and resource optimization
ai_impact.current_state: Maintenance planners manually create work orders based on condition reports, schedule crews using spreadsheets and EAM system
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI agent auto-generates work orders from condition assessments, optimizes priority based on risk and business impact, matches required skills to available crews, books optimal maintenance windows
ai_impact.estimated_value: 80% reduction in manual planning effort, 25% better resource utilization
barriers: Integration with multiple EAM systems across regions

value_streams.name: Design-to-Commission
value_streams.description: Solution design through installation, testing, energization, and substation/plant commissioning
value_streams.mermaid:
graph LR
  P016["Equipment\nInstallation"]
  P017["Commissioning\nTesting"]
  P018["Customer\nHandover"]
  P016 --> P017 --> P018
  classDef high fill:#fef2f2,stroke:#dc2626,color:#991b1b
  classDef medium fill:#fffbeb,stroke:#d97706,color:#92400e
  classDef low fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#16a34a,color:#166534
  class P017,P018 medium
  class P016 low

ai_impact.summary: AI-assisted commissioning with digital twin validation and automated documentation
ai_impact.current_state: 6-12 month commissioning cycle with sequential installation, testing, and handover phases — heavy documentation requirements per IEC 62271
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: Digital twin validates configuration before physical installation, AI assists test plan generation and results analysis, automated document assembly for handover package
barriers: Physical installation still requires skilled crews

ai_impact.summary: AR-guided installation with real-time quality verification
ai_impact.current_state: Field engineers follow detailed work instructions for physical equipment installation — experienced crews needed for HV connections and protection wiring
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AR overlay guides less experienced installers through procedures, computer vision verifies connection correctness, real-time comparison against digital twin to catch errors before energization
barriers: Harsh substation environment limits AR device usage

ai_impact.summary: AI automates test sequence execution and results analysis against standards
ai_impact.current_state: Protection engineers manually execute test plans — relay injection testing, trip time verification, interlocking checks per IEC standards
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI generates optimal test sequence from protection scheme, auto-analyzes results against IEC limits, compares with fleet-wide baselines, generates compliance reports
ai_impact.estimated_value: 40% faster testing phase, automated compliance documentation

ai_impact.summary: Automated handover package assembly with AI-powered completeness verification
ai_impact.current_state: Project managers manually compile handover documentation — as-built drawings, test certificates, O&M manuals, warranty terms — often delayed by missing documents
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI agent assembles handover package from PLM, test systems, and project data, verifies completeness against contract requirements, auto-generates training materials from technical documentation

value_streams.name: Quote-to-Cash
value_streams.description: Customer inquiry and quotation through contract negotiation, delivery, invoicing, and revenue recognition
value_streams.mermaid:
graph LR
  P019["Quote-to-\nOrder"]
  P020["Supplier\nQualification"]
  classDef high fill:#fef2f2,stroke:#dc2626,color:#991b1b
  classDef medium fill:#fffbeb,stroke:#d97706,color:#92400e
  class P019 high
  class P020 medium

ai_impact.summary: AI-driven configuration, pricing, and proposal generation for complex energy solutions
ai_impact.current_state: Sales engineers spend 2-4 weeks configuring technical solutions, manually calculating costs, and assembling proposals for complex transformer and grid projects
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI agent interprets customer requirements, auto-configures optimal solution from product catalog, generates pricing with margin optimization, produces professional proposal document
barriers: Complex product configurations need expert validation

ai_impact.summary: AI-powered supplier risk assessment and automated qualification workflows
ai_impact.current_state: Procurement team manually evaluates suppliers through questionnaires, site audits, financial checks, and quality certifications — 3-6 month qualification cycle
ai_impact.ai_opportunity: AI agent continuously monitors supplier risk signals (financial health, ESG scores, news sentiment), auto-scores against qualification criteria, recommends approval with risk assessment
ai_impact.estimated_value: 50% faster qualification, continuous risk monitoring vs point-in-time
barriers: Supplier data availability varies by region
Audit requirements still need physical presence

technique_frequency.description: Virtual representation of physical assets or processes for simulation and validation
ML-based identification of unusual patterns in sensor, test, or operational data
Image and video analysis for quality inspection, defect detection, and AR guidance
Forecasting outcomes using historical data and ML models
Automated generation and assembly of reports, certificates, and documentation
Auto-generated reports from test results, compliance data, and operational metrics
Traversal and analysis of connected data in knowledge graphs and grid topology models
Structured representation of domain knowledge with typed entities and relationships
Identifying recurring patterns in signals, events, or data streams
Solving multi-objective optimization problems subject to engineering and business constraints
ML inference on streaming data with sub-second latency requirements
AI-driven exploration of design space to generate optimal configurations
AI systems that independently create and manage work schedules and maintenance windows
Optimizing allocation of workforce, equipment, and materials across competing demands
Finding optimal transport routes considering physical and regulatory constraints

methodology_note: This analysis maps AI impact to the Energy Client's actual business processes as modeled in the EA Repository. Each process is linked to its performing roles, supporting applications, and accessed data objects through ArchiMate-typed relationships. The AI impact assessment considers both the theoretical potential and the practical readiness given the current application landscape and organizational maturity. For the theoretical framework on how EA processes evolve in agentic organisations, see the Processes page.
